merge.diff:
diff --git a/src/jinja2/ext.py b/src/jinja2/ext.py
index d21b83aa..e5dff220 100644
--- a/src/jinja2/ext.py
+++ b/src/jinja2/ext.py
@@ -50,6 +50,10 @@ GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS: t.Tuple[str, ...] = (
 )
 _ws_re = re.compile(r"\s*\n\s*")
 
+#: parameter names in a ``{% trans %}`` tag that carry translation
+#: workflow metadata rather than template variables.
+_TRANS_METADATA_KEYS = ("note", "created", "modified")
+
 
 class Extension:
     """Extensions can be used to add extra functionality to the Jinja template
@@ -362,9 +366,10 @@ class InternationalizationExtension(Extension):
         plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
         plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
         variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
+        metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
         trimmed = None
         while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
-            if variables:
+            if variables or metadata:
                 parser.stream.expect("comma")
 
             # skip colon for python compatibility
@@ -372,13 +377,36 @@ class InternationalizationExtension(Extension):
                 break
 
             token = parser.stream.expect("name")
-            if token.value in variables:
+            if token.value in variables or token.value in metadata:
                 parser.fail(
                     f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                     token.lineno,
                     exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                 )
 
+            if token.value in _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS:
+                if parser.stream.current.type != "assign":
+                    parser.fail(
+                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a"
+                        " value assignment.",
+                        token.lineno,
+                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
+                    )
+                next(parser.stream)
+                value_expr = parser.parse_expression()
+                if not (
+                    isinstance(value_expr, nodes.Const)
+                    and isinstance(value_expr.value, str)
+                ):
+                    parser.fail(
+                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a"
+                        " string literal.",
+                        token.lineno,
+                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
+                    )
+                metadata[token.value] = value_expr.value
+                continue
+
             # expressions
             if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                 next(parser.stream)
@@ -459,6 +487,7 @@ class InternationalizationExtension(Extension):
             plural_expr,
             bool(referenced),
             num_called_num and have_plural,
+            metadata,
         )
         node.set_lineno(lineno)
         if plural_expr_assignment is not None:
@@ -514,6 +543,7 @@ class InternationalizationExtension(Extension):
         plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
         vars_referenced: bool,
         num_called_num: bool,
+        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
     ) -> nodes.Output:
         """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
         newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
@@ -568,7 +598,307 @@ class InternationalizationExtension(Extension):
                         ]
                     ),
                 )
-        return nodes.Output([node])
+        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
+        if metadata:
+            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
+        return output_node
+
+
+class ConditionalI18nExtension(Extension):
+    """Adds a ``{% ctrans %}`` tag that conditionally renders a
+    translation, falling back to literal content when the condition is
+    false.
+
+    .. code-block:: html+jinja
+
+        {% ctrans show_translation %}
+            Hello, {{ name }}!
+        {% fallback %}
+            Hi, {{ name }}!
+        {% endctrans %}
+
+    The condition is evaluated at render time, *before* the translation
+    functions run, so the untranslated fallback branch never touches
+    ``gettext``/``ngettext`` at all.
+    """
+
+    tags = {"ctrans"}
+
+    def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
+        """Parse a conditionally translatable tag."""
+        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
+        condition = parser.parse_expression()
+        num_called_num = False
+
+        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
+        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
+        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
+        trimmed = None
+        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
+            if variables:
+                parser.stream.expect("comma")
+
+            # skip colon for python compatibility
+            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
+                break
+
+            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
+            if token.valu

    def test_conditional_trans_with_pluralization(self):
        from jinja2.ext import InternationalizationExtension, conditional_i18n as ConditionalI18nExtension
        env = Environment(extensions=[InternationalizationExtension, ConditionalI18nExtension])
        env.globals.update({
            "gettext": lambda x: f"TRANSLATED: {x}",
            "ngettext": lambda s, p, n: f"TRANSLATED: {s if n == 1 else p}",
        })
    
        # Test with pluralization
>       tmpl = env.from_string(
            "{% ctrans use_i18n, count %}{{ count }} item{% pluralize %}{{ count }} items{% fallback %}{{ count }} thing(s){% endctrans %}"
        )
